1.description: (Color online) Deep Galerkin Method NN Architecture Used in This Paper Notes. (a) Components: input, hidden layers, and output of DGM NN. We show four hidden layers here, including one initial hidden layer and three LSTM-like layers ( L = 3). (b) A closer look into the hidden layer.

2.description: (Color online) RMSE Against Epoch for Different λ L Across Various Times t Under the Black-Scholes-Merton Model Notes. The RMSEs are calculated using the NN approximate TPDF benchmarked against the exact TPDF Equation (28) . This figure plots RMSE against epoch for different 𝜆 𝐿 = 0 . 1 , 1 , 1 0 , 1 0 0 , 1 , 0 0 0 λ L = 0.1 , 1 , 10 , 100 , 1 , 000 across various times 𝑡 = 0 . 2 5 t = 0.25 , 0.5, 0.75, 1.0 set up under the Black-Scholes-Merton model. The epoch checkpoints are 5,000, 25,000, 50,000, 100,000, 250,000, 500,000, 1,000,000, 1,500,000, and 2,000,000. Each checkpoint represents the NN with the smallest training loss up to that epoch checkpoint. The dashed line in the graph means no better NN was found and we use the best NN in the previous checkpoint. The domain Q used to train the NN is 𝑥 , 𝑦 ∈ [ − 2 . 3 , 2 . 3 ] x , y ∈ [ − 2.3 , 2.3 ] , 𝜎 ∈ [ 0 , 0 . 6 ] σ ∈ [ 0 , 0.6 ] , 𝑡 ∈ [ 0 , 1 . 2 ] . t ∈ [ 0 , 1.2 ] . In terms of validation, for each t , 𝜎 = 0 . 1 σ = 0.1 , 0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.3, 0.35, 0.4, 0.45,0.5, 0.55, 0.6. The initial log price 𝑥 0 = 0 x 0 = 0 , and the range of y for each ( 𝑡 , 𝜎 ) ( t , σ ) set is [ − 2 . 3 , 2 . 3 ] [ − 2.3 , 2.3 ] .

3.description: (Color online) Gaussian TPDF: Closed Form vs. NN Approximation Notes. The second row shows the same results in log scale of density. The figures shown correspond to the parameters 𝑥 = 0 x = 0 , 𝜎 = 0 . 2 σ = 0.2 , the time to maturity 𝑡 = 0 . 2 5 , 0 . 5 , 0 . 7 5 , 1 . 0 t = 0.25 , 0.5 , 0.75 , 1.0 , and 𝜆 𝐿 = 1 0 0 λ L = 100 . The domain used to train the network is 𝑥 , 𝑦 ∈ [ − 2 . 3 , 2 . 3 ] x , y ∈ [ − 2.3 , 2.3 ] , 𝜎 ∈ [ 0 , 0 . 6 ] σ ∈ [ 0 , 0.6 ] , 𝑡 ∈ [ 0 , 1 . 2 ] . t ∈ [ 0 , 1.2 ] .

4.description: (Color online) RMSE Against Epoch for Different λ L Across Various Small Times t Under the Black-Scholes-Merton Model Notes. The RMSEs are calculated using the NN approximate TPDF benchmarked against the exact TPDF Equation (28) . This figure plots RMSE against epoch for different 𝜆 𝐿 = 0 . 1 λ L = 0.1 , 1, 10, 100, 1,000 across various times 𝑡 = 0 . 0 2 5 t = 0.025 , 0.05, 0.075, 0.1 set up under the Black-Scholes-Merton model. The epoch checkpoints are 5,000, 25,000, 50,000, 100,000, 250,000, 500,000, 1,000,000, 1,500,000, and 2,000,000. Each checkpoint represents the NN with the smallest training loss up to that epoch checkpoint. The dashed line in the graph means no better NN can be found and we use the best NN in the previous checkpoint. The dotted line for 𝜆 𝐿 = 1 0 0 λ L = 100 represented the fine-tuned model after 500 epochs. The domain Q used to train the network is 𝑥 , 𝑦 ∈ [ − 2 . 3 , 2 . 3 ] x , y ∈ [ − 2.3 , 2.3 ] , 𝜎 ∈ [ 0 , 0 . 6 ] σ ∈ [ 0 , 0.6 ] , 𝑡 ∈ [ 0 , 1 . 2 ] . t ∈ [ 0 , 1.2 ] . In terms of validation, for each t , 𝜎 = 0 . 1 σ = 0.1 , 0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.3, 0.35, 0.4, 0.45, 0.5, 0.55, 0.6. The initial log price 𝑥 0 = 0 x 0 = 0 , and the range of y for each ( 𝑡 , 𝜎 ) ( t , σ ) set is [ − 2 . 3 , 2 . 3 ] [ − 2.3 , 2.3 ] .

5.description: (Color online) The Exact Gaussian TPDF and the Neural TPDF Before and After Tuning for Small Time to Maturity Notes. The first row shows the exact Gaussian TPDF and the neural TPDF before and after tuning for small time to maturity. The second row shows the same results in log scale of density. 𝑥 = 0 x = 0 , 𝜎 = 0 . 2 σ = 0.2 , the time to maturity 𝑇 = 0 . 0 2 5 T = 0.025 , 0.05, 0.075, 0.1. The NN results after 500 epochs tuning (and we select the best trained NN).

6.description: (Color online) Joint TPDF Graphs According to the Semiclosed Form and to the NN Approximation and Corresponding Marginal Densities Notes. The marginal densities are calculated according to Equation (32) . The NN is trained with 500,000 epochs. The parameters are 𝑥 = 0 x = 0 , 𝑣 = 0 . 2 v = 0.2 , 𝜅 = 1 . 0 κ = 1.0 , 𝜔 = 0 . 2 ω = 0.2 , 𝜉 = 0 . 2 ξ = 0.2 , 𝜌 = 0 . 2 ρ = 0.2 . Time to maturity 𝑇 = 1 . 0 T = 1.0 . For the NN approximation, 𝜆 𝐿 = 1 0 0 λ L = 100 . The joint TPDF is plotted using 200 points across both y and z axes. (a) True and neural joint TPDFs. (b) The corresponding marginal densities of 𝑋 ⁡ ( 𝑇 ) X ⁡ ( T ) calculated from the joint TPDFs and the same graph in log scale (right).

7.description: (Color online) RMSE Plot Against 𝜆 𝐿 λ L for the Heston Model with 𝜆 𝐿 = 1 , 1 0 , 1 0 0 , 1 , 0 0 0 λ L = 1 , 10 , 100 , 1 , 000 Notes. The setup is the same as in Table 3 . The mean result is calculated using the mean values of all four maturities across 𝜆 𝐿 λ L .

8.description: (Color online) Heston’s Volatility Smile Obtained Through Put Prices Under the Semiclosed Form TPDF and Neural TPDF Notes. Parameters used: 𝑆 0 = 1 , 𝑉 0 = 0 . 1 6 , 𝑇 = 1 . 0 , 𝜅 = 1 . 0 , 𝜔 = 0 . 3 , 𝜉 = 0 . 3 5 , 𝜌 = − 0 . 5 S 0 = 1 , V 0 = 0.16 , T = 1.0 , κ = 1.0 , ω = 0.3 , ξ = 0.35 , ρ = − 0.5 . We calculate option prices with 11 different strikes 𝐾 = 0 . 5 , 0 . 6 , … , 1 . 5 K = 0.5 , 0.6 , … , 1.5 .

9.description: (Color online) Comparison of Marginal Densities for the SABR Model Calculated by Different Methods Notes. The marginal densities are obtained through the following methods: prices calculated by the finite difference method implemented in QuantLib, the approximate marginal density for price calculated using the finite difference method ( Hagan et al. 2014 ), the approximate implied volatility formula from Hagan et al. (2002 ), and the marginal density obtained through the joint neural TPDF. The right-hand graph presents a comparison of the marginal densities in log scale. The parameters used for the SABR model are the initial price 𝐹 0 = 1 F 0 = 1 , the initial volatility 𝜎 0 = 0 . 3 5 , σ 0 = 0.35 , 𝛽 = 0 . 6 β = 0.6 , 𝜌 = − 0 . 1 ρ = − 0.1 , 𝑡 = 1 . 0 t = 1.0 . We use 201 points interpolation. The NN marginal densities are calculated by numerical integration from the joint neural TPDF.

10.description: (Color online) SABR Volatility Smile Obtained Through Call Prices Using the QUAD Method with TPDF Computed Using Finite Difference Methods and with the Neural TPDF, Monte Carlo Simulations, and Approximate Implied Volatility Formula Notes. The parameters used: 𝐹 0 = 1 , 𝜎 0 = 0 . 3 5 , 𝑇 = 1 . 0 , 𝛽 = 0 . 6 , 𝜌 = − 0 . 1 F 0 = 1 , σ 0 = 0.35 , T = 1.0 , β = 0.6 , ρ = − 0.1 . We calculate option prices for 11 strikes 𝐾 = 0 . 5 , 0 . 6 , … , 1 . 5 K = 0.5 , 0.6 , … , 1.5 .

11.description: (Color online) True TPDF vs. Neural TPDF for Kou’s Double Jump Exponential Model Notes. The first row shows the true TPDF of Kou’s double jump exponential model calculated by inverse Fourier transform of the characteristic function (IFT) vs. the neural TPDF (NN). The second row shows the same graph in log scale. The initial price is 𝑥 = 0 x = 0 and the other parameters used are similar to the ones from ( Kou 2002 ) (but under a forward measure): 𝜎 = 0 . 4 σ = 0.4 , 𝜆 = 1 . 0 λ = 1.0 , 𝑝 = 0 . 4 p = 0.4 , 𝑞 = 0 . 6 q = 0.6 , 𝜂 1 = 1 0 η 1 = 10 , 𝜂 2 = 5 η 2 = 5 . The time to maturity 𝑡 = 0 . 2 5 t = 0.25 , 0.5, 0.75, 1.0. The hyperparameter 𝜆 𝐿 = 1 0 0 λ L = 100 . The domain used to train the NN is 𝑥 , 𝑦 ∈ [ − 5 . 0 , 5 . 0 ] x , y ∈ [ − 5.0 , 5.0 ] , 𝜎 ∈ [ 0 , 0 . 5 ] σ ∈ [ 0 , 0.5 ] , 𝑡 ∈ [ 0 , 1 . 2 ] t ∈ [ 0 , 1.2 ] , 𝜆 ∈ [ 0 . 0 , 2 . 0 ] λ ∈ [ 0.0 , 2.0 ] , 𝑝 ∈ [ 0 . 0 , 1 . 0 ] p ∈ [ 0.0 , 1.0 ] , 𝜂 1 ∈ [ 1 . 1 , 2 0 . 0 ] η 1 ∈ [ 1.1 , 20.0 ] , 𝜂 2 ∈ [ 0 . 1 , 2 0 . 0 ] η 2 ∈ [ 0.1 , 20.0 ] . We plot the graphs with 100 points.

12.description: (Color online) Kou’s Double Exponential Jump Diffusion Volatility Smile Obtained Through Put Prices Using the Semiclosed Form European Option Pricing Formula ( Kou 2002 ) and the QUAD Method with Neural TPDF Notes. The parameters used: 𝑆 0 = 1 S 0 = 1 , 𝜎 0 = 0 . 1 6 σ 0 = 0.16 , 𝑇 = 1 . 0 T = 1.0 , 𝜆 = 1 . 0 λ = 1.0 , 𝑝 = 0 . 4 p = 0.4 , 𝜂 1 = 1 0 η 1 = 10 , 𝜂 2 = 5 η 2 = 5 . We calculate option prices for 11 strikes 𝐾 = 0 . 5 , 0 . 6 , … , 1 . 5 K = 0.5 , 0.6 , … , 1.5 .

13.description: (Color online) SVJ Model Marginal Density Comparison: Semiclosed Form vs. NN Notes. Comparison of the marginal density obtained through the neural joint TPDF with the semiclosed form marginal density. The log scale graph is shown on the right. The parameters used: initial stock price 𝑆 0 = 1 S 0 = 1 , 𝑉 0 = 0 . 1 6 V 0 = 0.16 , 𝜔 = 0 . 3 ω = 0.3 , 𝜉 = 0 . 3 5 ξ = 0.35 , 𝜅 = 1 . 0 κ = 1.0 , 𝜆 = 1 . 0 λ = 1.0 , 𝜇 𝐽 = − 0 . 1 μ J = − 0.1 , 𝜎 𝐽 = 0 . 2 σ J = 0.2 , 𝜌 = − 0 . 5 ρ = − 0.5 . The semiclosed form marginal TPDF used in this example follows Bates (1996) .

14.description: (Color online) SVJ Model Implied Volatility Smile Comparison: Semiclosed Form vs. NN Notes. The implied volatility smile is obtained through put prices under the QUAD method with semiclosed form joint TPDF and with the neural TPDF. The parameters used: 𝑆 0 = 1 S 0 = 1 , 𝑉 0 = 0 . 1 6 V 0 = 0.16 , 𝜔 = 0 . 3 ω = 0.3 , 𝜉 = 0 . 3 5 ξ = 0.35 , 𝜅 = 1 . 0 κ = 1.0 , 𝜆 = 1 . 0 λ = 1.0 , 𝜇 𝐽 = − 0 . 1 μ J = − 0.1 , 𝜎 𝐽 = 0 . 2 σ J = 0.2 , 𝜌 = − 0 . 5 ρ = − 0.5 . We calculate option prices for 11 strikes 𝐾 = 0 . 5 , 0 . 6 , … , 1 . 5 K = 0.5 , 0.6 , … , 1.5 .
